---
title: "Outputs"
toc: false
---

<!--
This page is for things you’ve produced and shared.
Not everything needs to be a paper.
If it exists, is citable, or is useful to others, it belongs here.
-->

## Publications

- Frankenstein, V. (2025). *Mapping tau-vulnerable cell populations using spatial transcriptomics.*  
  bioRxiv. https://biorxiv.org  

- Frankenstein, V., Igor, I. (2024). *Network models of protein aggregation in neurodegenerative disease.*  
  Journal of Computational Neuroscience.  

- Frankenstein, V. (2023). *When proteins misbehave: modelling pathological propagation in neural circuits.*  
  Neurobiology Reports.

---

## Talks & posters

- UK DRI Annual Conference (2024).  
  *When proteins misbehave: tracing tau across the brain.*

- International Conference on Neurodegeneration (2023).  
  *Spatial transcriptomics reveals vulnerable neuronal populations.*

- Computational Neuroscience Workshop (2023).  
  *Reproducible analysis pipelines for complex biological datasets.*

---

## Software, data & other resources

- **Tau Spatial Analysis Pipeline**  
  Reproducible R workflow for analysing spatial transcriptomics data in neurodegeneration research.  
  https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/tau-spatial-analysis  

- **Neurodegeneration Network Models**  
  Tools for modelling how pathological proteins propagate across neural circuits.  
  https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/neurodegeneration-networks  

- **Tau Pathology Benchmark Dataset**  
  Public dataset used to compare image analysis methods for measuring tau aggregation.  
  https://zenodo.org

---

## Other outputs (optional)

- **Reproducible Neuroscience Workshop (2024)**  
  Training materials for teaching version control and reproducible workflows to experimental researchers.

- **Public engagement: Brain donation and dementia research (2024)**  
  Outreach event explaining how donated brain tissue supports neuroscience research.

- **Guest lecture: “Ethics and ambition in experimental science” (2023)**  
  Seminar discussing historical and ethical perspectives on biological experimentation.
